item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description: ### Fixed - **Game launchers now place and close correctly.** Apps whose visible window is owned by a spawned helper or bootstrapper process — Steam (via "Steam Helper") and League of Legends (via "Riot Client") — used to open at their own remembered spot instead of the position you configured, and were left running when the scene changed. Their window is now tracked and positioned, and the whole app is torn down on the next Replace or Stop. - Closing a scene no longer hangs behind an app's "Are you sure you want to quit?" confirmation (ChatGPT and other Electron apps): a scene app this app launched is now force-quit, which skips the prompt.

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description:
### Added - **Grok** joins the built-in launch-target presets.
### Changed - **Stop now closes your scene.** Pressing Stop used to leave every launched window behind with no way to clean them up later; it now tears the open scene down the same way a Replace does. - The **Replace** scene mode is now labeled beta in Settings (it stays the default).
### Fixed - Launched terminal windows no longer end up on a hidden desktop Space or the wrong display — they open on the Space you are looking at and land at their configured position, including on multi-display setups. - **Ghostty**: repeated launches no longer keep adding split panes to your own windows, and scene windows now close cleanly on Replace. - Closing a scene no longer hangs behind a terminal's "terminate running processes?" confirmation — the prompt is answered automatically. - An app that was already running when a scene launched it (music players, chat apps, …) is no longer left behind: Replace and Stop now close just the window the scene used, without ever quitting the app itself. - One unlaunchable target no longer breaks the rest of its scene — the other apps still launch, get tracked, and are cleaned up on the next Replace.

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description:
### Added - Launch targets now follow a "one target, one window" model. When a target has several URLs or commands, they open together as tabs or split panes inside that one window instead of scattering across the screen. Want another window? Just add the same app again. - New **Sensor** settings tab for tuning wake-word detection.
### Changed - Wake-word listening is now pinned to your built-in microphone, so plugging in or switching audio devices no longer interrupts detection. - Terminal commands now run in an interactive login shell, so your `PATH`, aliases, and shell configuration apply just like they do in a normal terminal.
### Fixed - Launched windows now land where they should — several placement glitches with tabs, splits, and duplicate targets are fixed. - Setup Agent now asks for the automation and launch permissions it needs right from the model settings screen, instead of a target quietly failing to open the first time.

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
### Added - First-launch onboarding now surfaces the microphone and accessibility permission prompts automatically. Previously, because Setup Agent lives only in the menu bar, a fresh install opened no window and you had to find Settings yourself before it could start listening. - The installer disk image now opens a styled drag-to-Applications window, so installing is a single drag.
### Changed - Wake-word models are now stored in an app-managed location. Models saved by an earlier version are migrated automatically the first time you launch this build — no action needed.
### Fixed - After updating, macOS may ask you to re-grant Microphone, Accessibility, and Automation permissions once. This is expected: the app now uses a stable new identity, and the new onboarding will walk you through granting them.

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description: ### Fixed - The app now launches reliably from the packaged build. SwiftPM resource bundles are resolved from the app's `Contents/Resources`, fixing a startup failure where the previous build could fail to load its bundled resources.

item.title: Version 1.0.1
item.description: ### Changed - First release published to the public **SetupAgent** distribution repo (signed, notarized DMG + Sparkle auto-update). Source stays in the private source repo.
